rulings.Case Title: EDWARD SHELTON VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 6, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. As to the causation issue, defendant sustained its initial burden. Mr. Shelton's deposition testimony and declaration and Charles Ay's declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Shelton was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation) attributable to defendant. As to the duty issue, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant owed a duty to Mr. Shelton. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it did not demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive written discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their claim that defendant owed Mr. Shelton a duty. As to the sophisticated user defense, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that such defense applies to bar plaintiffs' claims against it. Plaintiffs' objections to Dr. Richard Cohen's declaration are overruled as there was no violation of CRC 3.1115 or C.C.P. section 422.30. Defendant's objections to Mr. Ay's declaration are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD SHELTON VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 6, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Shelton was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Shelton's deposition testimony and plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. The deposition testimony of George Kirk is irrelevant as plaintiffs are not claiming exposure to Kaiser Gypsum drywall. Defendant's objections to plaintiffs' Exhibit A (Mr. Shelton's deposition testimony) are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THEODORE WINTER ET AL VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 6, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant BorgWarner Morse TEC Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is granted. As to summary judgment, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Winter was exposed to a product attributable to defendant. Plaintiff Theodore Winter's identification during his deposition of a BorgWarner clutch is admissible. (In re Cindy L. (1997) 17 Cal.4th 15.) The deposition testimony of Theodore Winter and Brad Winter preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. Even assuming the declaration of Mr. Merz is admissible, it merely creates a triable issue when read in conjunction with Brad Winter's deposition testimony and it has no bearing on Theodore Winter's deposition testimony. For summary judgment, product identification was the only argument made by defendant. As to summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages, plaintiffs failed to present clear and convincing evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. While plaintiffs do not need to prove their case for punitive damages at the summary adjudication stage, the Court takes the higher proof standard into consideration. (See American Airlines, Inc. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1017; Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48.) Plaintiffs' objections to Mr. Merz's declaration are overruled. Plaintiffs' objections to Richard Anderson's declaration are overruled. Defendant's objections to Brad Winter's deposition testimony are overruled. Defendant's objections to Plaintiffs' Exhibit J (1997 responses to standard interrogatories) are sustained. As discussed at the April 28, 2015 hearing, defendant is awarded the costs it incurred in drafting an additional reply due to plaintiffs' failure to file a timely opposition. That amount is $1,755.00. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
